summary: The Focus RS delivers incredible performance but has a notable head gasket concern on early cars that Ford addressed. The 2.3L EcoBoost is otherwise robust, and the AWD system is excellent.

issues.name: Head Gasket Failure
issues.description: Early Focus RS models (2016–2017) can develop head gasket failures, causing coolant loss, white exhaust smoke, and overheating. Ford issued a recall and revised the head gasket design.

issues.name: Rear Differential Mount Failure
issues.description: The rear differential mount can fail under hard launches, causing clunking and drivetrain vibration. A revised mount and aftermarket options exist.

issues.name: Intercooler Condensation
issues.description: In humid conditions, the top-mount intercooler can accumulate condensation that gets ingested by the engine, causing misfires and hesitation.

issues.name: Clutch Judder
issues.description: The factory clutch can develop judder during engagement, particularly on cars used for hard launches. Fluid contamination on the clutch disc is often the cause.

issues.name: Wheel Bearing Noise
issues.description: Front wheel bearings can develop a humming noise at highway speeds. The AWD system puts additional stress on the bearings.

buyingTips: Verify the head gasket recall (19S12) has been completed — this is the single most critical item on any Focus RS
Late 2017 and 2018 models are preferred — they shipped with the revised head gasket and other improvements
Check coolant level and look for white exhaust smoke — signs of head gasket issues that may not have been addressed
The Drift Mode is cool but hard on the drivetrain — check rear differential mount and axle condition
Only available as a manual transmission — verify clutch engagement is smooth with no judder or slipping
